title: [InstCombine] Canonicalize `abs(sub(ext(X),ext(Y)))` -> `ext(sub(max(X,Y),min(X,Y)))`
summary: The developers generalized an optimization for `abs(sub(ext(X), ext(Y)))` by recognizing a pattern where the absolute difference of extended values can be expressed as a zext of the unsigned difference between min/max of the original operands. They identified two variants: signed extension (`sext`) uses `smax`/`smin`, while zero extension (`zext`) uses `umax`/`umin`. The transformation ensures single-use of the sub operation to avoid increasing instruction count and requires matching operand types. This approach replaces a multi-instruction sequence (ext, sub, abs) with a more efficient min/max-based difference, improving codegen for loops computing absolute differences. The key steps are: match the pattern, select the appropriate min/max intrinsic based on extension type, compute the difference, and zero-extend the result. This pattern is now reusable across any code matching `abs(sub(ext(X), ext(Y)))`.
